description: Serotonin accumulation leading to serotonin syndrome (potentially fatal). Minimum 14-day washout required (5 weeks for fluoxetine).
Serotonergic synergy increasing risk of serotonin syndrome.
CYP1A2 inhibition and vitamin K disruption causing excessive bleeding risk.
Antiplatelet and anticoagulant synergy. GI bleeding risk doubles.
Reduced potassium excretion leading to hyperkalemia and cardiac arrhythmia risk.
CYP3A4 inhibition reduces statin metabolism. Risk of rhabdomyolysis (muscle breakdown leading to renal failure).
Additive AV node depression causing severe bradycardia or heart block.
Additive AV node depression causing severe bradycardia or heart block.
Contrast-induced nephropathy may reduce metformin clearance, risking lactic acidosis. Hold if eGFR <30 or AKI risk.
Additive CNS depression causing respiratory depression, potentially fatal.
Additive CNS depression causing respiratory depression, potentially fatal.
NSAIDs reduce lithium renal clearance by 25-40%, causing lithium toxicity (narrow therapeutic index).
Thiazides increase lithium reabsorption. Lithium levels rise 25-40%, risking toxicity.
Amiodarone inhibits P-glycoprotein and renal clearance, doubling digoxin levels. Mandatory 50% dose reduction.
Reduced renal and hepatic digoxin clearance leading to digoxin toxicity.
Hypokalemia potentiates digoxin toxicity, causing cardiac arrhythmia at otherwise therapeutic levels.
Valproate inhibits epoxide hydrolase, causing toxic carbamazepine-10,11-epoxide accumulation. Neurotoxicity despite normal carbamazepine levels.
